question: Which Washington county had the lowest 2020 census population among those counties that (1) had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in every school year from 2018-19 through 2021-22, (2) had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in the 2021-22 school year, and (3) were not the most populous county in Washington? Only report the county name in the format of `X County`. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore.config import Config
from botocore import UNSIGNED

source = "datagov/report-card-enrollment-2018-19-school-year/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
s3 = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))
obj = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

filtered = df[
    df["OrganizationLevel"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq("district")
    & df["GradeLevel"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq("all grades")
].copy()
filtered["County"] = filtered["County"].astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(r"\s+", " ", regex=True)
filtered = filtered[filtered["County"].ne("") & filtered["County"].str.lower().ne("nan")].copy()
filtered["All Students"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["All Students"].astype(str).str.replace(",", "", regex=False), errors="coerce")
filtered["Low-Income"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["Low-Income"].astype(str).str.replace(",", "", regex=False), errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered.dropna(subset=["All Students", "Low-Income"])

county_totals = filtered.groupby("County", dropna=True)[["All Students", "Low-Income"]].sum().reset_index()
county_totals = county_totals[county_totals["All Students"].gt(0)].copy()
county_totals["LowIncomePct"] = county_totals["Low-Income"] / county_totals["All Students"] * 100
answer = county_totals.loc[county_totals["LowIncomePct"].between(32, 40), "County"].sort_values().tolist()
1.subquestion: Which Washington counties had Low-Income student enrollment percentage between 32-40% in the 2018-19 school year?

5.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore.config import Config
from botocore import UNSIGNED

source = "datagov/report-card-assessment-data-2021-22-school-year/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
candidate_counties = ["King", "Kitsap", "San Juan", "Whitman"]
s3 = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED))
obj = s3.get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

filtered = df[
    df["OrganizationLevel"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq("district")
    & df["StudentGroup"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq("all students")
    & df["GradeLevel"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq("all grades")
    & df["TestSubject"].astype(str).str.strip().str.lower().eq("math")
].copy()
filtered["County"] = filtered["County"].astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(r"\s+", " ", regex=True)
filtered = filtered[filtered["County"].isin(candidate_counties)].copy()
filtered["PercentMetStandard"] = pd.to_numeric(filtered["PercentMetStandard"].astype(str).str.replace("%", "", regex=False).str.replace(",", "", regex=False), errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered.dropna(subset=["PercentMetStandard"])

county_means = filtered.groupby("County", dropna=True)["PercentMetStandard"].mean()
answer = county_means[county_means.gt(40)].sort_index().index.tolist()
5.subquestion: Among {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman} (from hop 1), which counties had district-average Math proficiency above 40% in 2021-22?

6.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for King County, Washington, 'The population was 2,269,675 in the 2020 census, making it the most populous county in Washington.'
6.subquestion: Among {King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman} (from hop 1), which counties are NOT the most populous in Washington?

7.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Kitsap County, Washington, 'As of the 2020 census, its population was 275,611.'
7.subquestion: What was Kitsap County's 2020 census population?

8.fact: According to the Wikipedia article for Whitman County, Washington, 'As of the 2020 census, the population was 47,973.'
8.subquestion: What was Whitman County's 2020 census population?

reasoning: Node 1: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2018-19, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county and calculating Low-Income percentage (Low-Income / All Students * 100), the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: Island (39.4%), King (34.7%), Kitsap (39.2%), San Juan (37.8%), Snohomish (39.6%), and Whitman (37.6%).

Node 2: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2019-20, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county, the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: Island (38.7%), King (34.2%), Kitsap (38.3%), San Juan (37.6%), Snohomish (39.3%), and Whitman (37.7%).

Node 3: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2020-21, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county, the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: Island (36.4%), King (34.1%), Kitsap (35.1%), San Juan (34.6%), Snohomish (38.3%), Thurston (39.4%), and Whitman (34.7%).

Node 4: According to the Washington Report Card Enrollment data for 2021-22, aggregating district-level 'All Grades' data by county, the following counties had Low-Income percentage between 32-40%: King (35.9%), Kitsap (38.0%), San Juan (38.2%), and Whitman (39.3%). Note: Island had 40.3% and Snohomish had 41.8%, both exceeding the 40% threshold.

Derive hop 1 answer using nodes 1, 2, 3, 4: Nodes 1-4 ask for counties with low-income enrollment percentage between 32% and 40% in each school year from 2018-19 to 2021-22; intersecting these yearly sets yields King, Kitsap, San Juan, and Whitman. Hop answer: King, Kitsap, San Juan, Whitman.

Node 5: According to the Washington Report Card Assessment data for 2021-22, averaging district-level PercentMetStandard for Math (All Students, All Grades) by county: King had 43.5%, Kitsap had 44.4%, San Juan had 35.2%, and Whitman had 41.4%. Counties with district-average Math proficiency above 40% among hop 1 result: King, Kitsap, Whitman.
Node 6: According to the Wikipedia article for King County, Washington, 'The population was 2,269,675 in the 2020 census, making it the most populous county in Washington.'

Derive hop 2 answer using nodes 5, 6: Node 5 keeps counties from hop 1 with 2021-22 district-average Math proficiency above 40%, and node 6 removes Washington's most populous county, resulting in the candidate comparison set. Hop answer: Kitsap and Whitman.
Node 7: According to the Wikipedia article for Kitsap County, Washington, 'As of the 2020 census, its population was 275,611.'
Node 8: According to the Wikipedia article for Whitman County, Washington, 'As of the 2020 census, the population was 47,973.' Also, 'The county seat is Colfax.'

Derive hop 3 answer using nodes 7, 8: Nodes 7-8 provide 2020 populations for Kitsap and Whitman; comparing these values and taking the lower population yields the final county answer. Hop answer: Whitman County.
